title: iris ruthenica subsp. brevituba
intro: Iris ruthenica subsp. brevituba, also known as a type of bearded iris, is a plant species that belongs to the iris family (Iridaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe and Asia, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Iris ruthenica subsp. brevituba include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which are believed to help alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to promote digestion, relieve stress, and boost the immune system.
